subject: English
question:
Pablo Picasso showed his truly exceptional talent from a very young age. His first word was lapiz (Spanish for pencil) and he learnt to draw before he could talk. He was the only son in the family and very good-looking, so he was thoroughly spoilt. He hated school and often refused to go unless his doting parents allowed him to take one of his father’s pet pigeons with him. Apart from pigeons, his great love was art and when in 1891 his father, who was an amateur artist, got a job as a drawing teacher at a college, Pablo went with him to the college. He often watched his father paint and sometimes was allowed to help. One evening his father was painting a picture of their pigeons when he had to leave the room. He returned to find that Pablo had completed the picture, and it was so amazingly beautiful and lifelike that he gave his son his own palette and brushes and never painted again. Pablo was just thirteen.
\nQ1. As a boy Pablo Picasso was
explanation: The passage explicitly states he was 'the only son in the family and very good-looking' (handsome) and possessed 'truly exceptional talent'.

subject: English

\nQ2. He was spoilt mostly because he was

option.C: the only son in the family.

explanation: The passage states, 'He was the only son in the family and very good-looking, so he was thoroughly spoilt.'

option.D: he was allowed to carry a pet with him.

explanation: The passage notes that he 'often refused to go unless his doting parents allowed him to take one of his father’s pet pigeons with him.'

subject: English
question:
Pablo Picasso showed his truly exceptional talent from a very young age. His first word was lapiz (Spanish for pencil) and he learnt to draw before he could talk. He was the only son in the family and very good-looking, so he was thoroughly spoilt. He hated school and often refused to go unless his doting parents allowed him to take one of his father’s pet pigeons with him. Apart from pigeons, his great love was art and when in 1891 his father, who was an amateur artist, got a job as a drawing teacher at a college, Pablo went with him to the college. He often watched his father paint and sometimes was allowed to help. One evening his father was painting a picture of their pigeons when he had to leave the room. He returned to find that Pablo had completed the picture, and it was so amazingly beautiful and lifelike that he gave his son his own palette and brushes and never painted again. Pablo was just thirteen.
\nQ5. Pablo’s father gave up painting because he
explanation: The passage suggests that upon seeing Pablo's 'amazingly beautiful and lifelike' painting, the father gave his son his own tools and stopped painting himself, implying he recognized his son's superior artistic ability.

subject: English
question: Q6. The boss was irritated (a) / by him neglecting(b) / the duties and (c) / not listening to his advice(d)
explanation: The gerund 'neglecting' should be preceded by the possessive case 'his' instead of the objective case 'him'. Therefore, 'by his neglecting' is grammatically correct.

subject: English
question: Q7. Each of the three (a) / beggars were(b) / asking for more (c) / food to eat(d)
explanation: When the subject is 'Each', the verb must be singular. Thus, 'were' should be replaced with 'was'.

subject: English
question: Q8. My brother sent (a) / two pairs(b) / of shoe (c) / from America(d).
explanation: The word 'shoe' should be in the plural form 'shoes' when referring to the object in the pair.

subject: English
question: Q9. The young boy said (a) / that he(b) / neither liked me (c) / nor my wife(d).
explanation: The correlative conjunction 'neither... nor' must be placed symmetrically. It should be 'liked neither me nor my wife'.

subject: English
question: Q10. He was (a) / congratulated for(b) / his success in (c) / the 100 m race(d)
explanation: The standard preposition used with 'congratulated' is 'on', not 'for'.

subject: English
question:
Directions (11-15): Pick up the most effective word from the given words to fill in the blanks to make the 
\nQ11. Sanjay was______________ with divine vision to see the great battle.
explanation: To be 'endowed with' means to be provided or gifted with a quality or ability. It fits perfectly in the context of receiving a vision.

subject: English
question:
Directions (11-15): Pick up the most effective word from the given words to fill in the blanks to make the 
\nQ12. There was so much__________ material in the essay that it was difficult to get the author’s message.
explanation: 'Extraneous' means irrelevant or unrelated to the subject, which explains why the message was hard to find.

subject: English
question:
Directions (11-15): Pick up the most effective word from the given words to fill in the blanks to make the 
\nQ13. The world is so constructed that if you wish to enjoy its pleasures, you must also______ its pains.
explanation: In this context, 'endure' is the most logical word as it means to undergo or suffer through something difficult.

subject: English
question:
Directions (11-15): Pick up the most effective word from the given words to fill in the blanks to make the 
\nQ14. Indian press did not give ____________ to the British publicity.
explanation: 'Credence' means belief in or acceptance of something as true. The sentence suggests the press did not accept the British publicity.

subject: English
question: Q15. Travelers ____________their reservations well in advance if they want to travel during the Diwali holidays.
explanation: The phrase 'had better' is a modal construction used to express strong advice. It is always followed by the base form of the verb (bare infinitive). Thus, 'had better get' is the correct grammatical form.

subject: English
question: Q16. EMANCIPATE
explanation: To emancipate means to set free from legal, social, or political restrictions. Hence, 'Set free' is the correct synonym.

subject: English
question: Q17. DECEIT
explanation: Deceit refers to the action or practice of deceiving someone by concealing or misrepresenting the truth. 'Dishonesty' is the most similar term.

subject: English
question: Q18. ADMONITION
explanation: An admonition is an act or action of admonishing; authoritative counsel or warning. 'Warning' is the correct synonym.

subject: English
question: Q19. VOCIFEROUS
explanation: Vociferous means expressing feelings or opinions in a very loud or forceful way. The opposite of loud or noisy is 'Quiet'.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q20. IMPLICATE
explanation: To implicate means to show something to be involved in a crime or to connect to a situation. 'Involve' is the closest synonym.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q21. The person who is regarded as the greatest law giver of ancient India is
explanation: Manu is traditionally regarded as the author of the Manusmriti, the most foundational legal text of ancient India.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q22. The immortal fame of Ashoka largely rests upon

option.A: his conversion to Buddhism and its propagation.
option.B: his policy for the welfare of his subjects.
option.C: his work in the sphere of politics and moral teaching.

explanation: Ashoka is most remembered for his pivotal conversion to Buddhism and his subsequent efforts to propagate the religion across his empire and beyond.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q23. Gandhiji started Dandi March

option.A: to demonstrate against the British Empire.

explanation: The Dandi March, also known as the Salt Satyagraha, was a direct action campaign of tax resistance and nonviolent protest against the British salt monopoly.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q24. The first war of Indian Independence began on 10 May 1857 at
explanation: The revolt of 1857 broke out at Meerut on 10 May 1857 when the soldiers refused to use the greased cartridges.

subject: Geography
question: Q25. The sun rises in Arunachal Pradesh two hours before it does in Dwaraka in Gujarat. This is because the former is

option.A: higher in elevation than Dwaraka and the earth rotates from West to East.
option.B: situated further North than Dwaraka and the earth rotates from West to East.
option.C: situated further East (about 30° Longitude) than Dwarka and the earth rotates from West to East.
option.D: situated about 30º East of Dwaraka and the earth rotates from West to East.

explanation: The earth rotates $360^\circ$ in 24 hours, which means $15^\circ$ per hour. A difference of 2 hours corresponds to $30^\circ$ of longitude difference ($2 \times 15^\circ = 30^\circ$). Arunachal Pradesh is about $30^\circ$ East of Gujarat.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q26. Srinagar is situated on the banks of the river
explanation: Srinagar, the summer capital of Jammu and Kashmir, is located on the banks of the Jhelum river.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q27. The areas in India that receive approximately an average of more than 200 cms of rainfall annually are
explanation: High rainfall regions in India (receiving > 200 cm annually) include the North-Eastern states like Meghalaya, Assam, Nagaland, and Arunachal Pradesh due to the influence of monsoon winds hitting the hills.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q28. Isotherms are imaginary lines drawn on a map which connect places of equal
explanation: Isotherms are lines on a map connecting points that have the same temperature at a given time or on average over a given period.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q29. Jim Corbett National Park is situated in which state?
explanation: Jim Corbett National Park is located in the state of Uttarakhand (referred to as Uttaranchal at the time of the paper).

subject: General Awareness
question: Q30. Which one of the following statements regarding the sun is correct?

option.A: The sun is composed mainly of hydrogen.
option.B: Its energy is generated by nuclear collision in its interior.
option.C: It is calculated that the sun consumes about a trillion pounds of hydrogen every second.

explanation: The sun is primarily hydrogen and helium, generating energy through nuclear fusion (often described as collisions in simple contexts) at its core, and consumes vast amounts of hydrogen.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q31. Supersonic speed is speed greater than the speed of sound (in air at sea level) that is to say around______ miles/hour.
explanation: The speed of sound in dry air at $20^{\circ}C$ is approximately 767 miles per hour. 760 miles/hour is the standard approximate value used in general aviation contexts for speed at sea level.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q33. The National Institute of Oceanography is located at
explanation: The National Institute of Oceanography (NIO) is a research institution headquartered at Dona Paula, Panaji, Goa.

subject: General Science
question: Q34. An airplane rises because

option.B: the density of air above the plane is less than below it.
option.C: the pressure above its wings is less than the pressure below them.

explanation: According to Bernoulli's principle, the shape of an airplane's wing (airfoil) causes air to travel faster over the top surface than the bottom, creating a pressure difference (lower pressure above, higher pressure below) which generates lift.

subject: Geography
question: Q35. Rocks formed on the solidification of molten matter are called
explanation: Igneous rocks are formed through the cooling and solidification of magma or lava.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q36. The pioneer of Atomic energy in India is
explanation: Dr. Homi Jehangir Bhabha is known as the father of the Indian nuclear programme and was the founding director of the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR).

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q37. Which one of the following statements regarding FIFA World Cup 2010 is not correct?

option.A: South Africa became the first host nation to fail to qualify for the tournament’s second round.
option.B: The tournament was the culmination of a qualification process that began in August 2007.
option.C: This is the first time that the tournament was hosted by an African nation.
option.D: Zakumi, the official mascot for the FIFA World Cup, 2010 is an African bush elephant.

explanation: Zakumi, the official mascot for the 2010 FIFA World Cup, was a leopard, not an African bush elephant.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q38. The first Indian to win the World Amateur Snooker Championship is
explanation: Om Agarwal won the IBSF World Amateur Snooker Championship in 1984, becoming the first Indian to achieve this feat.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q39. The first person to win the Arjuna award for badminton is
explanation: Nandu Natekar was the first Indian badminton player to win the Arjuna Award, receiving it in the year 1961.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q40. The sport which requires the largest field is
explanation: Polo is played on the largest field among the given sports. A standard polo field is 300 yards long and 160 yards wide, which is significantly larger than the field dimensions for football, cricket, or hockey.

subject: English
question:
Q41. Find the odd one out.
(a) Advice
(b) Counsel
(c) Direct
(d) Suggest
explanation: Advice, Counsel, and Suggest are all forms of giving recommendations or opinions, whereas 'Direct' refers to giving an order or instruction.

subject: English
question:
Q42. Find the odd one out.
(a) Tumble
(b) Topple
(c) Crumble
(d) Sprain
explanation: Tumble, Topple, and Crumble all relate to a fall or collapse. A 'Sprain' refers to an injury to a joint or ligament.

subject: English
question:
Q43. Find the odd one out.
(a) Sobriquet
(b) Alias
(c) Pseudonym
(d) Anonymous
explanation: Sobriquet, Alias, and Pseudonym are all nouns referring to a name. 'Anonymous' is an adjective meaning having no name or unknown identity.

subject: General Knowledge
question:
Q44. Find the odd one out.
(a) Mumbai
(b) Goa
(c) Visakhapatnam
(d) Thiruvananthapuram
explanation: Mumbai, Visakhapatnam, and Thiruvananthapuram are all major cities. Goa is a State of India.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q46. Hope: Despair
explanation: The relationship is of antonyms. 'Hope' is the opposite of 'Despair', just as 'Encourage' is the opposite of 'Dishearten'.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q47. Army: Logistics
explanation: An army requires logistics for its systematic operation, just as a business requires a strategy for its successful functioning.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q48. Bouquet: Flower
explanation: A bouquet is a collection of flowers, just as a chain is a collection of links.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q49. Revenge: Vendetta
explanation: Revenge and vendetta are synonyms, just as envy and jealousy are synonyms.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q50. Refine: Style
explanation: To refine is to improve the style of something, just as to retouch is to improve or adjust a photograph.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q51. Fear: Tremble
explanation: Fear causes one to tremble, similar to how heat causes one to perspire. It is a cause-and-effect relationship regarding involuntary physical reactions.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q52. Condone: Offence
explanation: To condone an offence is to forgive or overlook it. Similarly, to overlook an aberration is to disregard an irregularity or deviation.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q53. Plant: Flower
explanation: A plant produces a flower as part of its structure. Similarly, a face includes an eye as a part of its structure.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q54. Protoplasm: Cell
explanation: Protoplasm is the fundamental substance that makes up a cell. Similarly, elements are the fundamental substances that make up chemistry.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q55. Nalanda: Takshashila
explanation: Nalanda and Takshashila are both historical, equivalent institutions of higher learning. Venus and Mars are both planets in the solar system, representing a similar categorical pairing.

subject: Reasoning
question:
DIRECTIONS (Qs. 56-60) : In the following questions (Q56-60) the first word is related to the second in the same way as the third word is related to the fourth. In the given problems either the third or the fourth word is missing and is left blank. You have to choose the correct word to fill in the blank.
\nQ56. Foot is to Man as Hoof is to _______.
explanation: A man has a foot for locomotion; similarly, a cow has a hoof for locomotion.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q57. Broad is to Narrow as ________ is to Lane.
explanation: The relationship is of antonyms. 'Broad' is the opposite of 'Narrow'. Similarly, a 'Road' (a wide path) is the opposite of a 'Lane' (a narrow path).

subject: Reasoning
question: Q58. Back is to Backbone as Belly is to _________.
explanation: The relationship represents a prominent feature located on a body part. The backbone is the central structure of the back, and the navel is a central feature of the belly.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q59. Ankle is to Knee as Wrist is to _____________.
explanation: The relationship is of corresponding joints. The ankle is the lower joint in the leg corresponding to the knee (the upper joint). Similarly, the wrist is the lower joint in the arm corresponding to the elbow (the upper joint).

subject: Reasoning
question: Q60. Sting is to Bee as ______ is to Snake.
explanation: The relationship is of the weapon used by the animal to attack. A bee uses its 'Sting' to attack, while a snake uses its 'Fangs'.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q61. Hari’s income is 20% more than Madhu’s income. Madhu’s income is less than Hari’s income by
explanation: Let Madhu's income be $100$. Hari's income = $100 + 20% \text{ of } 100 = 120$. Madhu's income is less than Hari's by $(120 - 100) = 20$. Percentage less = $\frac{20}{120} \times 100 = \frac{1}{6} \times 100 = 16.66\%$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q62. A sum of money lent out at simple interest doubled itself in 20 years. In how many years will it triple itself?
explanation: Let the principal be $P$. Amount after $20$ years becomes $2P$. Interest earned = $2P - P = P$. Simple interest formula: $P = \frac{P \times R \times 20}{100} \Rightarrow R = 5\%$. To triple the amount, total amount = $3P$, interest required = $2P$. Using $SI = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100} \Rightarrow 2P = \frac{P \times 5 \times T}{100} \Rightarrow T = \frac{200}{5} = 40 \text{ years}$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Which of the following fractions are in ascending order?
explanation: Convert fractions to decimals: $\frac{3}{5} = 0.6$, $\frac{2}{3} \approx 0.66$, $\frac{7}{9} \approx 0.77$, $\frac{9}{11} \approx 0.81$, $\frac{8}{9} \approx 0.88$. Since $0.6 < 0.66 < 0.77 < 0.81 < 0.88$, option C is in ascending order.

subject: Mathematics
question: Find the sum of: $\frac{1}{9} + \frac{1}{6} + \frac{1}{12} + \frac{1}{72}$
explanation: Finding a common denominator of 72: $\frac{8}{72} + \frac{12}{72} + \frac{6}{72} + \frac{1}{72} = \frac{8 + 12 + 6 + 1}{72} = \frac{27}{72}$. Simplifying by dividing by 9: $\frac{27 \div 9}{72 \div 9} = \frac{3}{8}$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Find the average of all the numbers between 6 and 34 which are divisible by 5.
explanation: Numbers between 6 and 34 divisible by 5 are: 10, 15, 20, 25, 30. Sum = $10 + 15 + 20 + 25 + 30 = 100$. Number of terms = 5. Average = $\frac{100}{5} = 20$.

subject: Mathematics
question: The average of first 80 natural numbers is
explanation: Average of first $n$ natural numbers is $\frac{n+1}{2}$. For $n = 80$, average = $\frac{80+1}{2} = \frac{81}{2} = 40.5$.

subject: Mathematics
question: If the sum of a few numbers is 450 and their mean is 50 and if another number 100 is included, the mean would become
explanation: Initial count of numbers $n = \frac{450}{50} = 9$. New sum = $450 + 100 = 550$. New count = $9 + 1 = 10$. New mean = $\frac{550}{10} = 55$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q70. The difference between a number and its two-fifth is 510. What is 10% of that number?
explanation: Let the number be $x$. The given condition is $x - \frac{2}{5}x = 510$. Solving for $x$: $\frac{3}{5}x = 510 \implies x = 510 \times \frac{5}{3} = 850$. 10% of 850 is $0.10 \times 850 = 85$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q71. One litre of water is evaporated from 6 litres of a solution containing 5% salt. The percentage of salt in the remaining solution is
explanation: Initial solution volume = 6 litres. Salt concentration = 5%. Amount of salt = $0.05 \times 6 = 0.3$ litres. After evaporation, the new volume = $6 - 1 = 5$ litres. The amount of salt remains 0.3 litres. New percentage = $(\frac{0.3}{5}) \times 100 = 6\%$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q72. A shopkeeper professes to sell all things at a discount of 10% but increases the selling price of each article by 20%. His gain on each article is
explanation: Let the original cost price be $CP = 100$. Initially, he might have intended to sell at $100$. By increasing the selling price (or marking price) by 20%, the new price is $120$. Applying a 10% discount on $120$ gives $120 - (0.10 \times 120) = 120 - 12 = 108$. Gain percentage = $(\frac{108 - 100}{100}) \times 100 = 8\%$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q73. If the selling price of an article is 4/3rd of its cost price, the profit in transaction is
explanation: Given $SP = \frac{4}{3} CP$. Profit = $SP - CP = \frac{4}{3} CP - CP = \frac{1}{3} CP$. Profit percentage = $(\frac{Profit}{CP}) \times 100 = (\frac{1/3 CP}{CP}) \times 100 = 33.33\%$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q74. If selling price is doubled, the profit triples. Find the profit percent.
explanation: Let $SP = S$ and $CP = C$. Profit $P = S - C$. If $SP$ becomes $2S$, profit becomes $3P$. So, $2S - C = 3(S - C) \implies 2S - C = 3S - 3C \implies 2C = S$. Profit $P = 2C - C = C$. Profit percentage = $(\frac{P}{C}) \times 100 = (\frac{C}{C}) \times 100 = 100\%$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q75. If 4/5th of an estate is worth Rs.16,800, then the value of 3/7th of the estate is
explanation: Let the total value of the estate be $x$. $\frac{4}{5}x = 16800 \implies x = 16800 \times \frac{5}{4} = 4200 \times 5 = 21000$. The value of $\frac{3}{7}$ of the estate = $\frac{3}{7} \times 21000 = 3 \times 3000 = 9000$.

subject: Mathematics
question: If $\frac{a}{b} = \frac{3}{4}$ and $8a + 5b = 22$, then the value of ‘a’ is
explanation: Given $\frac{a}{b} = \frac{3}{4}$, so $b = \frac{4a}{3}$. Substitute into $8a + 5b = 22$: $8a + 5(\frac{4a}{3}) = 22 \implies 8a + \frac{20a}{3} = 22 \implies \frac{24a + 20a}{3} = 22 \implies 44a = 66 \implies a = \frac{66}{44} = 1.5$. Re-checking the equation: if $a=1$, then $b=4/3$, $8(1)+5(4/3) = 8 + 6.66 \neq 22$. If $a=1.5$, $b=2$, $8(1.5)+5(2) = 12+10=22$. The correct value is $1.5$ or $3/2$.

subject: Mathematics
question: If $(a - b)$ is 6 more than $(c + d)$ and $(a + b)$ is 3 less than $(c - d)$, then the value of $(a - c)$ is
explanation: From the problem: (1) $a - b = c + d + 6$ and (2) $a + b = c - d - 3$. Adding (1) and (2): $2a = 2c + 3 \implies a - c = 1.5$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Two numbers are in the ratio 7: 8. If 3 is added to each of them their ratio becomes 8: 9. The numbers are
explanation: Let numbers be $7x$ and $8x$. Then $\frac{7x + 3}{8x + 3} = \frac{8}{9}$. Cross-multiplying: $9(7x + 3) = 8(8x + 3) \implies 63x + 27 = 64x + 24 \implies x = 3$. Numbers are $7(3)=21$ and $8(3)=24$.

subject: Mathematics
question: The sum of three numbers is 98. If the ratio of the first to the second is 2: 3 and that of the second to the third is 5: 8, then the second number is
explanation: Ratio $A:B = 2:3 = 10:15$. Ratio $B:C = 5:8 = 15:24$. Thus $A:B:C = 10:15:24$. Sum of parts $= 10+15+24 = 49$. Value of one part $= 98/49 = 2$. Second number $B = 15 \times 2 = 30$.

subject: Mathematics
question: A certain amount was divided between Sita and Gita in the ratio 9: 8. If Sita’s share was Rs. 4500 then the amount is
explanation: Sita's share ratio = 9, Gita's share ratio = 8. $9x = 4500 \implies x = 500$. Total amount = $9x + 8x = 17x = 17 \times 500 = 8500$.

subject: Reasoning
question:

(x) a @ 6) @)
(a) 1
(b) 2
(c) 3
(d) 4
explanation: This is a pattern-based reasoning question where the missing segment must complete the sequence or matrix provided in the visual prompt.

explanation: Identify the logical transformation or geometric completion required to fit the missing position in the figure set.

explanation: The series follows a logical progression of geometric shapes. Option 1 represents the correct continuation of the pattern.

question:

\nQ86. 
(X) (1) (2) (3) (4)
explanation: This is a pattern completion question where the missing segment of figure (X) must be identified from the given options (1), (2), (3), or (4) to maintain the logical symmetry or sequence of the pattern.

question:

\nQ87. 
(X) (1) (2) (3) (4)
explanation: This is a pattern completion question where the missing segment of figure (X) must be identified from the given options (1), (2), (3), or (4) to maintain the logical symmetry or sequence of the pattern.

subject: Reasoning
question:

subject: English
subtopic: Impact of computer technology on society
question:
The development and widespread use of computer technology and the internet have transformed how we 
communicate, how business is conducted, how information is dispersed, and how society is organised. 
Prior to 1980, in-depth information about any one subject matter was attained through laborious research 
involving countless visits to libraries and via repeated interviews with persons of known reputation and 
reputable expertise. Now, a great deal of information is available at the click of a mouse button, all 
attainable from within the confines of one's own home or from the use of a computer in an office. Previous 
labour-intensive support Jobs. such as loading and unpacking of trucks', luggage handling at airports, and 
food manufacturing, once performed by a large middle-class workforce, are now performed routinely by 
robots which are monitored by computer-controlled systems. Our lives have been simplified but these 
benefits which have been ushered in by the technology revolution have had an adverse effect on the core 
of our interpersonal-relationships. Mere communication is no longer via postal mail or face-to-face contact, 
but rather via electronic email, personal internet message boards and by virtue of hand-held personal 
electronic assistants. Although computer technology has brought us to within a mouse click of any sought-
after piece of information, this technology boom has sequestered us to the confines of our computer desks 
and homes and has removed us away from those traditional settings where personal and communication 
skills are developed. 
 
Q1. The author's attitude the advent of computer technology can be best summarised as
explanation: The author acknowledges the benefits and transformation brought by technology (appreciative) but points out the adverse effects on interpersonal relationships and traditional settings (reserved/cautious).

Q2. The author would agree with which of the following statements?

option.A: The advent of computer technology has decreased access to libraries
option.B: Because of advancements in robotics, labour-intensive jobs are more plentiful
option.C: Although heralded as a great leap forward, the widespread use of computer technology is not without \nits setbacks
option.D: Of all the benefits ushered in by the use the internet. electronic email is the most beneficial

explanation: The author discusses how technology has simplified life but also notes that it has had an adverse effect on interpersonal relationships and social development, implying it has both benefits and setbacks.

Q3. The author's primary purpose in writing this passage is most likely which of the following?

option.A: To downplay the need for the internet
option.B: To explain how robotics and the internet have had both a positive and negative influence on how we \nlive
option.C: To pave way for the next great technology revolution
option.D: To showcase the wonders of recent technology advancements

explanation: The passage provides a balanced view, acknowledging the transformation of information access and automation (positives) while simultaneously critiquing the loss of interpersonal and communication skills (negatives).

subject: English
question:
DIRECTIONS (Qs. 4-6): In each of the following choose the word most similar in meaning to the word given in capitals.
\nQ4. OSMOSIS
explanation: Osmosis refers to the process of gradual or unconscious assimilation of ideas, knowledge, or influence, similar to the process of diffusion.

subject: English
question:
DIRECTIONS (Qs. 4-6): In each of the following choose the word most similar in meaning to the word given in capitals.
\nQ5. OSTRACISE
explanation: To ostracise means to exclude someone from a society or group, which is synonymous with cutting them off.

subject: English
question:
DIRECTIONS (Qs. 4-6): In each of the following choose the word most similar in meaning to the word given in capitals.
\nQ6. ALACRITY
explanation: Alacrity means brisk and cheerful readiness, which is best represented by the word eagerness.

subject: English
question:
DIRECTIONS (Qs. 7-10): In each of the following choose the word most nearly opposite in meaning to the word given in capitals.
\nQ7. PROFUSION
explanation: Profusion means an abundance or large quantity of something. Scarcity is its opposite, meaning a shortage.

subject: English
question:
DIRECTIONS (Qs. 7-10): In each of the following choose the word most nearly opposite in meaning to the word given in capitals.
\nQ8. ESCHEW
explanation: To eschew means to deliberately avoid or abstain from using. Therefore, 'use' is the most nearly opposite term.

subject: English
question:
DIRECTIONS (Qs. 7-10): In each of the following choose the word most nearly opposite in meaning to the word given in capitals.
\nQ9. ABSTAIN
explanation: To abstain means to restrain oneself from doing or enjoying something. To indulge is to allow oneself to enjoy the pleasure of something.

subject: English
question: Q10. INSOLENT
explanation: Insolent means showing a rude and arrogant lack of respect. Affable means friendly, good-natured, or easy to talk to, which serves as the best contextual opposite among the choices.

subject: English
question: Q11. If you will (a) / follow my instructions (b) / you will get (c) / a suitable reward for this (d)
explanation: In a conditional sentence starting with 'If', the future tense (will) is not used in the 'if' clause. The correct structure is 'If you follow my instructions'.

subject: English
question: Q12. Harshad, along with (a) / his brother (b) / Ashwani and six senior officials (c) / were arrested (d)
explanation: When subjects are joined by 'along with', the verb should agree with the first subject ('Harshad'). Therefore, 'were' should be replaced by 'was'.

subject: English
question: Q13. He received timely support (a) / from his elder brother (b) / who is working abroad (c) / for the last six years (d)
explanation: Because the sentence indicates a duration of time ('for the last six years'), the present perfect continuous tense ('has been working') should be used instead of 'is working'.

subject: English
question: Q14. One of the drawbacks (a) / of modern education are (b) / that it does not encourage original thinking (c) / No error (d)

option.C: that it does not encourage original thinking

explanation: In the construction 'One of the...', the verb must be singular because 'one' is the subject. Therefore, 'are' should be 'is'.

subject: English
question: Q15. Morphine and other (a) / narcotic drugs are valuable (b) / medically, if misused (c) / it can cause irreparable damage (d)
explanation: The subject 'Morphine and other narcotic drugs' is plural, so the pronoun referring to it must also be plural. 'It' should be replaced by 'they'.

subject: English
question: Q16. His book was marked by many ______ remarks which made us forget its main theme.
explanation: A 'digressive' remark is one that moves away from the main subject, which fits the context of making one 'forget its main theme'.

subject: English
question: Q17. Some people have the capacity for learning foreign languages but they have no ________ to speak.
explanation: The sentence structure implies a contrast between the capacity to learn and the practical execution of speaking, making 'ability' the correct word in the context of skill or competence.

subject: English
question: Q18. The dispute among the parties became so _______ that there was every likelihood of a free exchange of blows.
explanation: The word 'acrimonious' refers to angry and bitter feelings in a speech or debate, which fits the context of a heated dispute likely to lead to blows.

subject: English
question: Q19. The judge decided to resign when he was _______ for promotion to Chief Justice.
explanation: The phrasal verb 'passed over' means to ignore someone or to not give them an opportunity, especially for promotion.

subject: English
question: Q20. Questions will be answered by a _______ of experts.
explanation: A group of experts who are brought together to discuss or evaluate something is called a 'panel'.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q21. Who among the following had discovered the Bramhi Script in 1838?
explanation: James Prinsep, an officer in the mint of the East India Company, deciphered the Brahmi script in 1837-1838.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q22. Who was known as father of administration in medieval India?
explanation: Sher Shah Suri is often credited as the father of administration in medieval India for his extensive reforms in land revenue, currency, and administration systems.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q23. Brahma Samaj was founded by
explanation: The Brahmo Samaj was founded in 1828 by Raja Rammohan Roy as a monotheistic movement of Hinduism.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q24. Who discharges the function of the President when vacancy occur In the office of President & Vice President simultaneously, owing to removal, death, resignation or th incumbent or otherwise?
explanation: According to the President (Discharge of Functions) Act, 1969, if the offices of both the President and the Vice-President fall vacant simultaneously, the Chief Justice of India (or in his absence, the seniormost judge of the Supreme Court) acts as the President.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q25. With which sport is Lewis Hamilton associated?
explanation: Lewis Hamilton is a British racing driver who competes in Formula One (F-1).

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q26. From which country does the top seeded Tennis player Rafael Nadal hail from?
explanation: Rafael Nadal is a professional tennis player from Spain.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q27. Which Indian Boxer won the gold medal in 60 kg category in Asian game and became the youngest to win a boxing gold for India?
explanation: Vikas Krishan Yadav won the gold medal in the 60 kg weight category at the 2010 Asian Games held in Guangzhou, China, becoming a notable young boxing champion for India.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q28. In 2010 Leander Paes in partnership with Cara Black won the mixed doubles title of
explanation: In 2010, Leander Paes and Cara Black won both the Australian Open and the Wimbledon mixed doubles titles.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q29. Term 'Prime Vertical' in astronomy is defined as

option.A: the vertical circle which passes through the east and west point of the horizon and is at right angles to the meridian of the place.
option.B: the vertical circle which passes through the north and south pain of he horizon and is at right angles to the meridian of the place.

explanation: In observational astronomy, the prime vertical is the vertical circle that passes through the zenith and the east and west cardinal points of the horizon.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q30. MNREGA stands for

option.A: Mahatma Gandhi National Revenue Engagement Guarantee Association
option.B: Maharaja National Revenue Employment Guarantee Act
option.C: Mahanagar National Rural Employment Guarantee Act
option.D: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act

explanation: MNREGA stands for Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act, which is a labour law and social security measure that aims to guarantee the 'right to work'.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q31. River Jhelum emerges from

option.A: Northern slopes of the Kailash range

explanation: The Jhelum River originates from a spring at Verinag, which is situated in the south-eastern part of the Kashmir Valley.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q32. Haematite ores is the ore of which metal?
explanation: Haematite is one of the most important ores of iron with the chemical formula $Fe_2O_3$.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q33. Green revolution relates to which of the following?

option.C: Self-dependence in petroleum crude oil production

explanation: The Green Revolution refers to a period of time when agriculture in India was converted into an industrial system due to the adoption of modern methods and technology, resulting in self-dependence in foodgrain production.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q34. Nuclear Submarine Akula has been handed over to India recently by
explanation: INS Chakra, an Akula-class nuclear-powered submarine, was leased to India by Russia.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q35. On which among the following dates, the Army Day is celebrated?
explanation: Army Day in India is celebrated on 15 January every year in recognition of Lieutenant General K. M. Cariappa's taking over as the first Commander-in-Chief of the Indian Army.

subject: General Science
question: Q36. Vertebrates have two endocrine glands associated with the brain, namely
explanation: In vertebrates, the pituitary gland and the pineal gland are both located within the brain and are key components of the endocrine system.

subject: General Science
question: Q37. The layer common to two adjacent plant cells called Middle Lamella is composed of
explanation: The middle lamella is a pectin-rich layer that acts as a cement between the primary cell walls of adjacent plant cells, primarily consisting of calcium pectate and magnesium pectate.

subject: General Science
question: Q38. With the increase of the effective nuclear charge, the size of the atom or ion

option.C: remain the same, since it has no bearing on size
option.D: it will depend on period to period and group to group

explanation: As the effective nuclear charge increases, the nucleus exerts a stronger attractive force on the electrons, pulling them closer and resulting in a smaller atomic or ionic radius.

subject: General Science
question: Q39. On which of the following statements, is the kinetic theory of matter base?

option.A: Matter is made up of molecules
option.C: Molecules experience forces of attraction between one another

explanation: The kinetic theory of matter is based on the postulates that matter consists of particles (atoms/molecules) in constant motion and that these particles interact through intermolecular forces.

subject: General Science
question: Q40. When heated with chloroform, secondary amines and tertiary amines
explanation: The carbylamine reaction is a test for primary amines only. Primary amines react with chloroform and a base to form isocyanides (carbylamines), while secondary and tertiary amines do not.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q41. The number whose square is equal to the difference of the squares of 40 and 32 is
explanation: Let the number be $x$. According to the problem, $x^2 = 40^2 - 32^2$. Using the identity $a^2 - b^2 = (a-b)(a+b)$, we get $x^2 = (40 - 32)(40 + 32) = 8 \times 72 = 576$. Thus, $x = \sqrt{576} = 24$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q42. 5 buckets of water fill a tank when the capacity of each bucket is 7 litres. How many buckets will be needed to fill the same tank, if the capacity of the bucket is 5 litres?
explanation: Total capacity of the tank = Number of buckets × Capacity per bucket = $5 \times 7 = 35$ litres. If the capacity of each bucket is 5 litres, then the number of buckets required = $\frac{35}{5} = 7$. Note: The options provided in the source text seem incorrect as the calculated result is 7.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q43. A vessel, full of water, weighs 27.5 kg. when the vessel is 1/4 full, it weighs 12.26 kg. Find the weight of empty vessel?
explanation: Let $V$ be the weight of the vessel and $W$ be the weight of water when full. $V + W = 27.5$. $V + \frac{1}{4}W = 12.26$. Subtracting the second from the first: $\frac{3}{4}W = 27.5 - 12.26 = 15.24$. So, $W = \frac{15.24 \times 4}{3} = 20.32$. Then $V = 27.5 - 20.32 = 7.18$ kg.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q44. If a + b = 10 and ab = 21, find the value of a^3 + b^3?

subject: Mathematics
question: Q45. If $\frac{a}{a + b} = \frac{17}{23}$, what is $\frac{a + b}{a - b}$ equal to?
explanation: $\frac{a}{a + b} = \frac{17}{23} \implies 23a = 17a + 17b \implies 6a = 17b \implies a = \frac{17}{6}b$. Substituting in the expression: $\frac{\frac{17}{6}b + b}{\frac{17}{6}b - b} = \frac{\frac{23}{6}b}{\frac{11}{6}b} = \frac{23}{11}$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q46. Evaluate: $\frac{(598+479)^2-(598-479)^2}{(598 \times 479)}$
explanation: Using identity $(x+y)^2 - (x-y)^2 = 4xy$. Here, $\frac{4(598 \times 479)}{598 \times 479} = 4$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q47. A man’s average monthly expenditure for the first four months of the year was Rs. 225.25. For the next five months, the average monthly expenditure was Rs. 20.75 more than what it was during the first four months. If the person spent Rs. 700 in all during the remaining three months of the year, find what percentage of his annual income of Rs. 3500 he saved in the year?
explanation: Expenditure for first 4 months = $4 \times 225.25 = 901$. Expenditure for next 5 months = $5 \times (225.25 + 20.75) = 5 \times 246 = 1230$. Remaining expenditure = 700. Total annual expenditure = $901 + 1230 + 700 = 2831$. Savings = $3500 - 2831 = 669$. Savings percentage = $(\frac{669}{3500}) \times 100 \approx 19.11\%$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q48. The ratio of the present age of P and Q is 2:3. The ratio of their age after 18 years will be 4:5. What is the present age of Q?
explanation: Let the present ages of P and Q be $2x$ and $3x$ respectively. After 18 years, the ratio becomes: $\frac{2x + 18}{3x + 18} = \frac{4}{5}$. Cross-multiplying gives: $5(2x + 18) = 4(3x + 18)$, which simplifies to $10x + 90 = 12x + 72$. Rearranging, $2x = 18$, so $x = 9$. The present age of Q is $3x = 3 \times 9 = 27$ years.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q49. The mean temperature of Monday to Wednesday was 37°C and of Tuesday to Thursday was 34°C. If the temperature on Thursday was 4/5th that of Monday, the temperature on Thursday was
explanation: Let $M, Tu, W, Th$ represent temperatures. $(M + Tu + W) / 3 = 37 \Rightarrow M + Tu + W = 111$. $(Tu + W + Th) / 3 = 34 \Rightarrow Tu + W + Th = 102$. Subtracting equations: $M - Th = 111 - 102 = 9$. Given $Th = \frac{4}{5}M$, so $M = \frac{5}{4}Th$. Substituting: $\frac{5}{4}Th - Th = 9 \Rightarrow \frac{1}{4}Th = 9 \Rightarrow Th = 36$°C.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q50. Srinivasan invests two equal amounts in two banks giving 10% and 12% rate of interest respectively. At the end of year the interest earned is Rs. 1650. Find the sum invested in each.
explanation: Let the sum invested in each bank be $x$. Total interest earned: $x \times 0.10 + x \times 0.12 = 1650$. $0.22x = 1650$. $x = \frac{1650}{0.22} = 7500$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q51. The simple interest on sum of money is 1/9 of the sum. The number of years is numerically equal to the rate percent per annum. The rate percent per annum is
explanation: Let the sum be $P$ and time be $T$ years. Interest $I = \frac{1}{9}P$. Also $I = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100}$. Given $R = T$. Thus, $\frac{1}{9}P = \frac{P \times R^2}{100} \Rightarrow R^2 = \frac{100}{9} \Rightarrow R = \sqrt{\frac{100}{9}} = \frac{10}{3} = 3.33$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q52. A sum of Rs. 10.000 is lent partly at 8% and the remaining at 10% per annum, If the yearly interest on the average is 9.2%. The money lent at 10% is
explanation:
Using Alligation: 
Rate 1: 8%, Rate 2: 10%, Average Rate: 9.2%. 
Difference: $|10 - 9.2| = 0.8$ and $|9.2 - 8| = 1.2$. 
Ratio of money = $0.8 : 1.2 = 8:12 = 2:3$. 
Money at 10% = $ rac{3}{2+3} 	imes 10000 =  rac{3}{5} 	imes 10000 = 6000$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q53. In an election between two candidates, 60% of the voters cast their votes, out of which 4% of the votes were declared invalid. A candidate got 7344 votes which were 75% of the total valid votes. Find the total number of votes enrolled in that election.
explanation: Let the total number of votes enrolled be $x$. Voters who cast their votes = $0.6x$. Valid votes = $0.6x \times (1 - 0.04) = 0.6x \times 0.96 = 0.576x$. Given that 7344 is 75% of valid votes: $0.75 \times 0.576x = 7344$. $0.432x = 7344$. $x = \frac{7344}{0.432} = 17000$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q54. If the price of kerosene be raised by 9%, find how much percent a house holder must reduce his consumption of kerosene so that not to increase his expenditure
explanation: Let original price = 100, consumption = 100. Expenditure = 10000. New price = 109. To keep expenditure same, new consumption $C = \frac{10000}{109} \approx 91.74$. Percentage reduction $= 100 - 91.74 = 8.26\%$. The closest option is 8.25%. Formula: $\frac{R}{100+R} \times 100 = \frac{9}{109} \times 100 \approx 8.257\%$.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q55. Sixty five pupils from a school entered for an examination and 80% of them passed. Another school entered 10 more pupils than the first school and four more pupils passed. The % of pass in the second school was
explanation: School 1: 65 pupils, 80% passed = $65 \times 0.8 = 52$ passed. School 2: $65 + 10 = 75$ pupils, $52 + 4 = 56$ passed. Pass percentage = $(\frac{56}{75}) \times 100 = 74.66\%$. This is approximately 75%.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q56. A jar contains black and white marbles. If there are ten marbles in the jar, then which of the following could not be the ratio of black to white marbles?
explanation: The sum of the parts of the ratio must be a divisor of the total number of marbles (10). For 1:10, sum of parts = $1 + 10 = 11$. Since 10 is not divisible by 11, the ratio 1:10 is impossible for 10 marbles.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q57. A sum of money is to be distributed among P, Q and R in the ratio of 6:19:7. If R gives Rs. 200 from his share to Q, the ratio of P, Q and R becomes 3:10:3, what is the total sum?
explanation: Initial shares: $P = 6x$, $Q = 19x$, $R = 7x$. Total sum $= 32x$. After transfer: $P = 6x$, $Q = 19x + 200$, $R = 7x - 200$. New ratio: $\frac{6x}{19x+200} = \frac{3}{10}$. $60x = 57x + 600 \implies 3x = 600 \implies x = 200$. Total sum $= 32 \times 200 = 6400$. *Note: Re-evaluating ratio constraint: If $x=200$, $P=1200, Q=3800, R=1400$. R gives 200 to Q: $P=1200, Q=4000, R=1200$. Ratio $1200:4000:1200 = 3:10:3$. Total $= 6400$. Option C is 6400.

subject: Mathematics
question: Q58. In what ratio should tea worth Rs. 10 per kg be mixed with tea worth Rs. 14 per kg so that the average price of the mixture may be Rs. 11 per kg?
explanation: Using alligation rule: (Price 1 = 10, Price 2 = 14, Mean = 11). Ratio $= (14-11) : (11-10) = 3 : 1$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Q59. Calculate the amount on Rs. 1250 for 2 years at 4% per annum. compounded yearly.
explanation: The formula for the amount with compound interest is $A = P(1 + \frac{r}{100})^n$. Given $P = 1250$, $r = 4$, and $n = 2$. Substituting the values: $A = 1250(1 + \frac{4}{100})^2 = 1250(1 + 0.04)^2 = 1250(1.04)^2 = 1250(1.0816) = 1352$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Q60. A sum of Rs. 3200 is lent out into two parts, one at 6% and another at 4%. If the total annual income is Rs. 176, find the money lent at 6%.
explanation: Let the amount lent at 6% be $x$. Then the amount lent at 4% is $(3200 - x)$. According to the problem, the total interest is Rs. 176. So, $\frac{6x}{100} + \frac{4(3200 - x)}{100} = 176$. Multiplying by 100, we get $6x + 12800 - 4x = 17600$, which simplifies to $2x = 4800$, so $x = 2400$.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q61. Surgeon: Scalpel
explanation: A surgeon uses a scalpel as a primary tool for their work. Similarly, a sculptor uses a chisel as a primary tool.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q62. Creche: Infants
explanation: A creche is a place specifically for infants. Similarly, a school is a place specifically for pupils.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q63. Pesticide: Plant
explanation: Pesticide is used to protect/treat a plant. Vaccination is used to protect/treat a body.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q64 . Stare: Glance
explanation: To stare is to look intensely, while to glance is to look briefly. Similarly, to gulp is to swallow quickly or in large amounts, whereas to sip is to drink in small quantities. The relationship is based on intensity or degree.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q65. Fish: Aquarium
explanation: A fish is kept in an aquarium. Similarly, bees are kept in an apiary. This is a relationship of an animal/creature and its place of confinement or habitat management.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q66. Ecstasy: Pleasure
explanation: Ecstasy is an extreme form of pleasure. Similarly, rage is an extreme form of anger. The relationship is based on intensity.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q67. Necromancy: Ghosts
explanation: Necromancy is the supposed practice of communicating with the dead or spirits to gain information. Alchemy was the medieval practice of attempting to turn base metals into gold. Both refer to historical or mystical practices aimed at achieving specific results.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q68. Coin: Mint
explanation: A mint is a place where coins are manufactured. A brewery is a place where wine or beer is manufactured. This denotes a product and its production site.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q69. Oak: Coniferous
explanation: Oak is a type of tree that fits into a broad biological category. Similarly, animals can be categorized based on their diet, such as Carnivore. This analogy identifies a member and its class.

subject: Reasoning
question: Q70. Onam: Kerala
explanation: Onam is a famous festival associated with the state of Kerala. Similarly, Bhangra is a famous dance form associated with the state of Punjab. This represents a cultural element and its regional origin.
